evals.prompt: Create a learning document for this project
evals.expected_output: A FOR[username].md file inside a timestamped topic folder (<topic>-<YYYYMMDD-HHMM>/) in the project root, written entirely in Taiwan Traditional Chinese, covering project overview, technical architecture, codebase structure, technology stack, design decisions, lessons learned, and engineering thinking patterns. When the markdown-to-html skill is installed, a FOR[username].html readable version generated from the Markdown ships alongside it in the same folder.
expectations: FOR[username].md is created inside a timestamped topic folder (<topic>-<YYYYMMDD-HHMM>/) in the project root, not loose in the root

When the markdown-to-html skill is installed, FOR[username].html is also created in the same timestamped folder (the readable version); if it is not installed, the HTML is skipped silently and only the .md ships
Any HTML produced was generated by delegating to the markdown-to-html skill's converter, not hand-written
The HTML declares lang="zh-TW" and UTF-8, and renders the tables and Mermaid diagrams correctly
The document is written entirely in Taiwan Traditional Chinese (not Simplified Chinese)
Taiwan-specific terminology is used (e.g., 程式碼 not 代碼, 資料庫 not 數據庫)
All 7 required sections are present: 專案概述, 技術架構, 程式碼結構, 技術選型, 設計決策, 學習心得, 工程師思維
The writing style is engaging and uses analogies, not dry textbook language
Diagrams are written as ```mermaid fenced blocks with Chinese labels (rendered as vector diagrams in the HTML, natively on GitLab/GitHub in the .md)

evals.prompt: Help me understand the testing patterns and error handling in this codebase, write a learning doc focusing on those areas
evals.expected_output: A FOR[username].md file focused on testing patterns and error handling, written in Taiwan Traditional Chinese, saved inside a timestamped topic folder, with detailed sections on how tests are structured and how errors are managed in the project.
expectations: FOR[username].md is created inside a timestamped topic folder (<topic>-<YYYYMMDD-HHMM>/) in the project root, not loose in the root
When the markdown-to-html skill is installed, FOR[username].html is also created in the same timestamped folder (readable version generated from the Markdown); otherwise only the .md ships
The document is written entirely in Taiwan Traditional Chinese
The document emphasizes testing patterns and error handling as requested
Specific test file names, test patterns, and error handling strategies from the codebase are referenced
The document includes real examples from the codebase, not generic advice
